_comment: fighter-seed.json — manually maintained editorial fields merged with live UFCStats data at build time.

_schema.id: Number — stable internal ID used by localStorage keys; never change once set
_schema.ufcstats_url: String — direct UFCStats fighter detail URL. Required for scraper. Find via: ufcstats.com/statistics/fighters?char=X&action=fighter_list
_schema.ufcstats_name: String — name exactly as it appears on UFCStats (for opponent matching in history)
_schema.mods: String[] — up to 3 modifier tags from MOD_COLORS
_schema.striking_extended: Object — fields UFCStats summary page does not provide; keep in sync with real data
_schema.grappling_extended: Object — fields UFCStats summary page does not provide; keep in sync with real data
_schema.event_overrides: Array at root level — editorial weigh_in + judges per fight per event

fighters.trader_notes: Elite grappler, historically dominant TD defense (92%). Dangerous off back against walls. Southpaw creates layered complexity vs orthodox fighters. Single career loss at 22 years old.
High output, dangerous left hook. Struggles against elite grapplers (3 sub losses). Absorbs 5.53/min — high volume both ways. Heart and volume compensate for durability questions.
Underrated grappling, dangerous from guard. Iron chin enables unconventional exchanges. Never backed down from contact. Fights better when hurt — pressure fighter who weaponizes damage.
Flashy counter-striker, exceptional lateral movement. Limited wrestling (0.51 TD/15). Vulnerable to grapplers and pressure fighters. Front-runner — performs best early when fresh.
Highest TD volume in division at 7.33/15. Cardio freak — gets stronger every round. Late bloomer dynamic is real and tradeable. Early round striking is weakness before pace takes over.
Excellent BJJ, dangerous sub game from guard. Striking has real holes vs elite level. Heavy cutter flag. Big name public inflation — consistently overpriced due to fanbase size.

Most submissions in UFC history. Dangerous everywhere — sub threats from bottom, KO power standing. Chin issues exposed at elite LW level (4 KO losses). High-variance fighter; fights emotionally which amplifies both ceiling and floor.

Elite pressure and leg kick volume. Iron chin — rarely stops fighting when hurt. Gets better in deep water which creates late-round betting angles. Elite competition exposure (Khabib, Makhachev, Oliveira) keeps market price honest.

Legitimate title contender with elite TD volume and cardio. Contested Makhachev early in his career — rematch is the market event to watch. Late bloomer tag applies: pace and output have steadily risen. Underpriced relative to skillset in non-title fights.

Pressure grappler who controls pace and position. Decision machine — only 48% finish rate but controls every minute of fight time. Elite TD defense means he can dictate where the fight goes. Historically underrated; now correctly priced as champion.

KO of the year pedigree vs Usman — southpaw check hook is legitimate. Well-rounded but not elite in any single area; wins through composure and output. Rematch with Muhammad is the defining market. Historically underdog-priced before title wins.

Fastest KO rate in WW division. Reaches full output early — front-runner flag means rounds 1-2 are peak danger. Both career losses came by KO, suggesting chin questions at elite level not yet fully answered. Watch line when elite wrestlers are matched against him.

Generationally complete skillset — elite in every dimension simultaneously. Reach (84") and intelligence create near-unsolvable puzzles. Only technical loss (Hamill, 2009) was a DQ; effectively unbeaten. Father time is the only real market risk. Heavily favorited in all markets; line movement on Jones fights is rare and significant when it occurs.

Most dangerous submission artist in FLW. Triangle choke finisher from many positions. Southpaw stance creates angle problems for orthodox fighters. Former LFO prospect who grew into a complete champion. Chin is the question mark — has been stopped before, though rarely in UFC.

Elite submission game paired with high volume. Three-fight Pantoja rivalry defines the division. Incredible cardio — becomes more dangerous late in fights. Chin has been exposed in multiple Pantoja fights; volatility is the main market risk. Always competitive regardless of outcome.

Guillotine and leg lock specialist. Submission game is elite and unpredictable. High variance fighter — has been finished in several high-profile bouts. DURABILITY RISK flag applies; not suited for a slow technical chess match. Best value when priced as an underdog.

Underrated complete game with elite grappling foundation. Allstars camp brings Dagestani wrestling principles. Dangerous triangle and arm lock game from bottom. Patient, technical fighter who rarely makes mistakes. Market tends to undervalue him pre-fight.

Technical boxing-based game, strong power for the weight class. City Kickboxing camp ensures elite striking foundation. Emerging contender with potential to break into title picture. Less submission-oriented than typical FLW contenders — presents unique matchup problems for sub hunters.

Explosive knockout artist with elite first-round finishing rate. Front-runner flag: if he doesn't finish early, performance level drops. Chin concerns from early career stoppages. Strong public value when opponent is a slow starter or late-round fighter.

Young Dagestani-style grappler with relentless pace. TD volume and top control are his primary weapons. Still developing but already posting elite wrestling numbers. Late bloomer tag — ceiling is very high. Underpriced in early title contender fights.

Khabib's cousin, same Dagestani wrestling DNA. Elite grappling with emerging striking — the complete package. Never been finished in professional career. UFC career is still early but he is already a consensus top-3 BW. Fights with the same suffocating style as his cousin. Ceiling is title.

Most dynamic kickboxer in BW. Southpaw stance creates elite angle problems. Flying knee and spinning back kick finishes — unorthodox and dangerous. Lost to Sterling (sub) and Aljamain (title) but remains the best striker in division. Best value when paired against grapplers who bring him into deep waters.

Relentless forward pressure with devastating leg kicks and body work. Rare South American finisher with elite KO and sub capabilities. Durability allows him to take shots to land his own — high attrition style. Front runner flag is absent — gets stronger mid-fight. Public perception often undervalues him.

Former FLW champion who moved up to BW. Elite submission and KO finishing rate — rare combination. Weight cut flag is significant: moved from FLW to BW and the stress of that cut at the highest level is a variable. Known for being stopped in several title fights. Best when fresh and on volume.

Technical defensive fighter with elite counter-punching. One-punch KO power in either hand. AKA China produces disciplined, calculated fighters. Won't lose to inferior strikers — the risk is elite-level grapplers who can bypass his standup. Public perception is often low due to conservative style.

Former BW champion with elite wrestling and BJJ. Serra-Longo pedigree. Step-up concern applies at FW — the weight class move after losing BW title adds unknowns. Volume striker style remains effective. Often underestimated by market before establishing himself — pattern applies to new weight class too.

Most technically precise KO finisher in FW history. Undefeated in MMA with elite KO rate. Southpaw stance paired with right-hand lead creates confusing attack angles. Took Holloway and Volkanovski's best shots and KO'd both — chin is iron. Patient, composed under pressure. Never been in serious danger. Market may be pricing him at peak; watch for first serious grappler vs him.

Most impressive striking volume output in FW history — 12 title fight wins. Late bloomer trajectory continued well into his 30s. Cardio is legendary — gets sharper every round. Iron chin: took Poirier's clean shots and kept fighting, took Topuria's shots through the fight. Historically a slight underdog in title fights but dominant as champion. Best at top of round 3+.

Dominant FW champion before Topuria. Elite pressure and volume. Heavy cutter — walked in at BW. Front-runner tag: starts fast and dominates early rounds; danger if taken into championship rounds. Chin exposed by Islam Makhachev KO in LW superfight — questionable at elite striking level now. City Kickboxing ensures technical excellence.

Dynamic striker with unorthodox angles. Spinning techniques and explosive entries create matchup problems. Rising fast — already top-5 material. Young and still developing — ceiling high. Market may undervalue him against established names.

One of the most durable fighters in UFC FW — took Max Holloway's best shots in both fights and kept moving. Elite guillotine from standing and triangle from guard. Iron chin allows him to survive early adversity and find submissions late. Best value when underdog vs strikers who aren't elite grapplers.

Elite grappler from the Chechen system — disciplined, technical, and relentless. Long winning streak in UFC before first loss. Suffocating top control and pace. Below the radar for casual fans — market tends to undervalue him as an underdog. Watch for him vs pure strikers.

Long unbeaten UFC run before Giga Chikadze loss. Solid across all areas — not elite anywhere, but consistently effective. SBG fight IQ is a real asset. Market often treats him as an afterthought but he has elite-level consistency. Good value in moderate-risk matchups.

Explosive first-round pressure with elite KO power — among the most dangerous early-round fighters in LW. Chin is questionable after multiple stoppage losses at highest level. High-variance betting candidate: when he hits first, he wins spectacularly; when he eats shots early, he crumbles. Cardio is strong if he survives round 1. Best value when implied probability is >55% favorite.

Late bloomer who found his stride at LW after multiple weight class moves. Excellent BJJ paired with serviceable volume striking. Team Aldo pedigree means technical standup. Better at 155 than FW. Market has historically undervalued him coming off injuries. Watch leg lock and guillotine setups.

French grappler with explosive wrestling and power shots. Rapid UFC rise — legitimate contender emerging fast. AKA France training environment ensures elite wrestling principles. Late bloomer tag applies: still improving technically. Chin has been exposed vs top competition (TKO to Islam). High ceiling but still unproven at absolute top.

Left-hand precision remains elite — possibly the most efficient KO artist in UFC LW history. Massive public inflation: most overpriced fighter in any market he enters. Cardio Concern after ~2012 vintage and multiple years of inactivity. Chin deteriorated significantly — stopped multiple times in recent fights. Leg injury history. Bet against his inflated line whenever he returns.

Undefeated. Best WW prospect in years — elite in all areas simultaneously. Southpaw stance, KO power, and underrated grappling create a nearly unsolvable puzzle. Never been tested at full championship pace. Step-up concern implied: hasn't faced a full 5-round champion yet. When the line opens, take him early — market is still under-rating him.

Dominant WW champion for years. Elite wrestling and improved striking made him one of the best P4P fighters. Chin exposed by Leon Edwards KO (twice) — questionable at elite level now. Age is a factor. Best if underdog vs younger fighters — market may over-respect former champion status. Watch opening line carefully.

BJJ world champion whose standup has become a serious weapon. Southpaw left hook is devastating. ATT training means real ground-and-pound threat. Historically priced as a moderate underdog — represents consistent value. Best when opponent isn't elite on the ground.

Pressure grappler who controls position through volume and grinding. Decision machine — wins through accumulated control and volume. Below the radar for casual fans. Market treats him as a slight underdog vs. elite competition, but he consistently outperforms expectations. Good value when priced +150 or higher.

Dynamic striker with exceptional range management. Undefeated in UFC — fights have mostly been against lower-ranked competition. Front-runner flag: performs best when setting pace early. Hasn't been tested at full championship quality yet. Market likes him; step-up concern when matched vs. elite grapplers. One of the most polarizing picks in WW.

Massive KO power, especially with the overhand right. Pressure style works until he eats shots in return — durability risk in wars. Multiple notable stoppages both ways. High variance betting candidate: best value when underdog, worst when heavy favorite. Fortis MMA camp produces powerful, aggressive fighters.

Most unorthodox fighter in WW — spinning attacks, sidekicks, spinning backfists. Karate-based stance creates genuine problems for conventional boxers. Made the transition from Bellator to UFC successfully. Market undervalued him initially; now fairly priced. Watch for him vs aggressive pressure fighters who negate his footwork.

Iron chin, relentless jab-heavy forward pressure. Highest volume output in MW — fights at a pace that breaks cardio concerns. Front-runner tag: reaches full output immediately and rarely slows. Elite cardio means he maintains this pace for 5 full rounds. Beaten by Du Plessis via close decision — market often over-corrects against him. Best when opponent is a low-volume counter striker.

Generational kickboxer — technical precision and range management are world-class. Southpaw stance creates elite angle issues. Chin has been compromised — Pereira KO'd him (twice counting kickboxing) and Strickland dominated him on the feet. Public still over-rates him due to resume — best fade when heavily favored. City Kickboxing ensures technical excellence but age and wear are factors.

Former champion who defined the MW era. Elite pressure, clean boxing, and underrated wrestling. Two KO losses to Romero exposed chin vulnerabilities — questionable at absolute top. Elite cardio keeps him competitive across 5 rounds. Best value when underdog: market over-reacts to his recent losses. Dangerous for any opponent who can't maintain distance.

Elite physicality and raw power — most muscular MW in UFC. Cardio Concern is the single biggest tradeable flag: famous for gassing in championship rounds (lost to Adesanya after dominating rounds 1-4). Best bet: against him in rounds 4-5 when he's been tested on the feet. First-round power is genuine. Grappling is underdeveloped.

Unbeaten until Whittaker loss — elite wrestler with fight-ending power. Allstars camp provides elite technical base. Underrated chin — has been in contact but rarely hurt. Best matchup: vs pure strikers who can't defend the takedown. Weak spot: elite wrestlers who turn it into a grappling match. Market prices him near max favor in most fights.

High submission rate combined with strong volume output for a grappler. Sanford MMA camp means improved striking over early career. Consistent performer. Market undervalues him in matchups vs pure strikers — he has the BJJ depth to submit them. Good underdog value consistently.

Most powerful finish in MW (ex-HW, multiple fight-ending single shots). Late bloomer trajectory after dropping from HW. Chin issues emerged at elite level — been stopped cleanly. Best when opponent walks into his power zone. Good underdog value when matched vs slower starters.

Relentless pressure with finishing power. Jackson-Wink game plan execution makes him more dangerous than raw stats suggest. Chin has been tested — gets hurt but fights through it. High variance — either finishes or gets finished. Best underdog value when priced +200 or higher.

Technical southpaw striker with growing UFC reputation. MMA Factory (Paris) background ensures good striking fundamentals. Southpaw KO power is legitimate. Rapidly ascending in the rankings — market lags his actual level. Best value as underdog in striking matchups vs right-handed fighters.

World kickboxing champion who translated that game to MMA faster than any fighter in history. Northpaw power punch creates unorthodox problems — southpaw with a right-hand lead. KO rate is among the best in the division. Grappling is a genuine weakness — has been submitted. Best matchup: vs strikers who lack his power. Worst matchup: elite wrestlers who take him down repeatedly.

Elite southpaw power, especially overhand right and spinning entries. Vacated title due to mental health leave — respect that decision, but market impact is real. Chin is questionable — KO'd by Pereira. Best when given room to set up combinations at distance. Worst when pressured by wrestlers who eliminate the space needed for his game.

City Kickboxing product — same team as Adesanya, Holloway, Volkanovski. Southpaw power with exceptional speed for LHW. Relatively early in UFC career but already top-5 material. Market may undervalue CKB product given team track record. Grappling defense is a known question mark.

Patient, technical Dagestani striker and grappler. Southpaw stance creates elite angle problems. Elite cardio sustains output for 5 full rounds. Slow starts cost him the Jones fight draw — watch early rounds. Best when given time to find range. One of the most complete fighters in LHW — market still slightly undervalues him.

Most creative and unorthodox LHW in history — spinning attacks from every angle. Former champion who vacated due to injury. Chin is questionable — been stopped by Ankalaev and in danger multiple times. DURABILITY RISK applies: high-damage style leads to cumulative wear. Best when underdog: his chaos factor makes him competitive vs anyone. Trains independently — minimal camp influence, high self-motivation.

Technical kickboxer with high KO rate. SFC Vienna training ensures strong striking fundamentals. Knee injury suffered vs Jones remains a durability question. Relatively conservative style — best value as moderate favorite vs pure strikers.

Late bloomer who found his stride after years as a gatekeeper. Forward pressure combined with heavy hands. Late bloomer tag is validated by consistent improvement trajectory. Best value when underdog vs established top-10 opponents — he has surprising KO power for his odds.

Most technically refined HW striker in history — footwork and movement are unprecedented at heavyweight. Elite cardio makes him better than any other HW in championship rounds. MMA Factory pedigree. Weak spot is elite wrestling — Jones and Ngannou exposed his takedown defense. Best matchup: vs slow, power-based HWs who can't match his footwork.

Highest TD rate in HW — relentless wrestling with ground-and-pound. Chin is questionable — stopped multiple times (Ngannou x2, Aspinall, Lewis). Best when opponent lacks TD defense. Worst when matched vs KO artists who can catch him countering. Elevation Fight Team striking has improved his standup.

One of the most devastating first-round KO artists in HW history — 6 consecutive first-round finishes before Gane and Jones. Front-runner flag: MUST finish early or performance collapses. Chin exposed by Jones KO. Cardio Concern is real. Best bet: heavy underdog vs elite HWs, because his first-round danger is always real regardless of odds.

Tall technical kickboxer (6'7") with long punches and excellent leg kick volume. Range management is his primary weapon. Chin exposed by Lewis KO and others — questionable at elite level. Consistent top-10 performer who never quite breaks the top-3. Best value as slight underdog vs mid-tier HW strikers.

Elite first-round KO power — one of the biggest punches in HW. Front-runner flag is extreme: if he doesn't finish in round 1 or 2, he collapses. Stopped multiple times — chin concern is genuine. Cardio Concern flag validated: visibly fades by round 3. Best bet when heavy underdog — his first-round danger is always real regardless of opponent quality.

Elite submission game with strong wrestling — has finished multiple opponents via RNC from back. Flexible MW/HW who fights where needed. Still relatively untested vs top-5 HWs but his skill set is elite. Best matchup: vs strikers with limited grappling. Best value: when underdog vs established HW names.

Most KO wins in UFC history — single-shot power that can end any fight. Iron chin at heavyweight level. Cardio Concern is extreme — moves in slow motion by round 3. Best betting angle: Lewis underdog with significant round 1 power — always a live dog. Worst: taking him as a favorite in 5-round championship fights. The Black Beast pattern is the most reliable archetype in HW betting.

Most accomplished HW champion in UFC history (3 title defenses as champ). Elite cardio at heavyweight — gets better in later rounds (opposite of most HWs). Strong boxing base with underrated wrestling. Age and inactivity are now the key market factors. Best when underdog — market often prices him based on age rather than skill.

Highest finishing rate among elite HW contenders. Extremely fast for the weight class — elite cardio is a major edge over aging division. Knee injury history warrants monitoring (suffered during Blaydes fight at UFC 304). Jones unification fight is the market event; currently priced as underdog but skillset gap is smaller than public believes.
